calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-15-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Peter S. Stern for and with Patricia Ann Mullen, Petitioner (via Zoom).

Mr. Stern explained that the decedent was the brother of petitioner, and that the estate consists primarily of tangible personal property valued under $50,000 and a parcel of commercial real estate in San Jose. A transfer-on-death deed had previously been attempted but was invalid due to its application to commercial property. As a result, probate of the will was necessary.

The Court inquired whether Suzanne Effle or any other parties would object to the petition, noting that the petitioner was one of the witnesses of the will. Mr. Stern explained that the will was prepared and executed by the decedent without legal counsel and in a family setting. He asserted that there was no dispute among heirs, including Suzanne Effle, who had been properly noticed and was aligned with the petitioner's interests. The Court opted to waive any possible conflict.

There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated October 4, 2016, is admitted to probate. Patricia Ann Mullen is appointed as the Executor with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. A status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for July 28, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. If either petition is filed two weeks prior, no appearance is required on that date. Amy Harrington is appointed probate referee.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
